title: AWS Cloud Practitioner
description: Question batch for the Cloud Concepts domain.

questions.question: What is the main AWS advantage of elasticity?
questions.explanation: Elasticity lets capacity grow or shrink with demand.

questions.question: Which cloud model provides services to many customers over the internet?
questions.explanation: A public cloud serves many customers from provider-managed infrastructure.

questions.question: Which cost model is most associated with AWS?
questions.explanation: AWS pricing is primarily based on actual usage.

questions.question: Which AWS feature helps reduce time spent provisioning servers manually?
questions.explanation: Cloud resources can be provisioned quickly by customers when needed.

questions.question: Which term describes spreading workloads across multiple systems to improve uptime?
questions.explanation: High availability reduces the impact of individual component failures.

questions.question: What is a primary reason companies move from CapEx to OpEx with AWS?
answers: They pay for services as they use them
They must buy more physical servers
questions.explanation: AWS shifts much spending from upfront purchases to operational usage costs.

questions.question: Which AWS infrastructure concept provides isolated locations inside a Region?
questions.explanation: Availability Zones are separate facilities within a Region.

questions.question: Which AWS principle helps match resources to unpredictable traffic?
questions.explanation: Scalability allows workloads to handle changing traffic patterns.

questions.question: What is the AWS shared responsibility model about?
answers: Dividing security duties between AWS and the customer
Running workloads only in private clouds
questions.explanation: AWS secures the cloud, while customers secure what they run in it.

questions.question: Which scenario best fits the need for a second AWS Region?
questions.explanation: A second Region provides broader geographic resilience.

questions.question: Which cloud benefit helps teams experiment without long hardware procurement cycles?
questions.explanation: Cloud agility reduces time between idea and deployment.

questions.question: Which statement best describes fault tolerance?
answers: A system continues operating despite component failures
A system always uses one large server
questions.explanation: Fault tolerance is the ability to keep working even when some components fail.

questions.question: Why do customers benefit from AWS global infrastructure?
answers: They can place workloads closer to users and recovery sites
They must use one Region only
questions.explanation: AWS global infrastructure supports low latency and resilience choices.

questions.question: What does scalability describe?
answers: A system's ability to handle more or less load
questions.explanation: Scalability is the ability to adjust to workload changes.

questions.question: Which type of scaling adds more instances instead of making one instance larger?
questions.explanation: Horizontal scaling adds more resources side by side.

questions.question: Which type of scaling increases CPU or memory on one instance?
questions.explanation: Vertical scaling increases the size of a single resource.

questions.question: What does business agility in cloud computing usually mean?
answers: Faster response to new requirements and opportunities
questions.explanation: Cloud services help teams move faster when requirements change.

questions.question: Which description best fits infrastructure as code?
answers: Using spreadsheets as the only control plane
questions.explanation: Infrastructure as code treats infrastructure setup as managed definitions.

questions.question: Why is right-sizing important in AWS?
answers: It helps align resource size with workload needs
questions.explanation: Right-sizing reduces waste and improves efficiency.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of managed services from a cloud concepts perspective?
questions.explanation: Managed services let teams focus more on business value than routine operations.

questions.question: Which design improves resilience inside one Region for a web app?
questions.explanation: Multiple AZs reduce the impact of a single-facility failure.

questions.question: What is the key benefit of using automation in cloud operations?
questions.explanation: Automation improves speed, consistency, and repeatability.

questions.question: Which statement best describes cloud economics?
answers: Resources can be aligned more closely to actual demand
Only one support plan is allowed
questions.explanation: Cloud economics improve when capacity is matched to real usage.

questions.question: Why is multi-Region architecture more complex than multi-AZ architecture?
answers: It spans geographically separate Regions with more coordination
questions.explanation: Multi-Region adds extra design and operational complexity across Regions.

questions.question: Which approach best supports resilience for stateful cloud workloads?
answers: Designing for backup, replication, and recovery
questions.explanation: Stateful workloads need explicit recovery and resilience planning.

questions.question: Which model best fits workloads that run only at certain times and then shut down?
questions.explanation: Cloud consumption pricing is well suited to intermittent workloads.

questions.question: What is the main value of elasticity during seasonal traffic spikes?
answers: It reduces the need to size for peak all year
It prevents outages in every scenario
questions.explanation: Elasticity helps avoid paying for peak capacity during quiet periods.

questions.question: Which cloud concept is most closely tied to launching resources without waiting for procurement?
questions.explanation: On-demand provisioning speeds up access to needed resources.

questions.question: Why do many companies begin cloud adoption with variable or bursty workloads?
answers: These workloads benefit quickly from elastic capacity
questions.explanation: Variable workloads often show immediate value from cloud elasticity.

questions.question: Which AWS concept most directly supports global customer experience improvement?
answers: Deploying in Regions closer to users
questions.explanation: Deploying closer to users can lower latency and improve experience.
